patterns.name: Server-authoritative math; client is a pure renderer
patterns.summary: Across ALL games, RNG, reel selection, win evaluation, RTP and feature triggers run on a remote math server (https://*.gladiatorgames.io) reached through a minified slotify/connector. The client calls connector.play(action, bet) and receives a fully-resolved result (final grid views, per-cascade waves, win amounts, multipliers, scatter level-ups, free-spin rounds). The client NEVER computes payouts or RTP — it only animates server results. Generic skills must treat math-config and math-engine as a server/back-office concern, with a strict client/server JSON contract between them.
patterns.genericization: Define a generic 'spin result contract' interface (balance, wager.data.{baseGame,freeGame,totalWin}, per-wave gameView/winInfo). Skill = math-engine emits it, client/presentation consumes it. Keep RNG+evaluation in a server module that the math-config skill parameterizes.

patterns.name: RLE-encoded integer paytable, payout = value/10 × bet
patterns.summary: Server sends paytable as paytable[symbolId] = int[] indexed by win-count level. Payout multiplier = rawInt/10 (e.g. 120 -> 12.0x). Client run-length-decodes adjacent equal values into display ranges like '8','13-14','19+'. Static JSON paytables in game/config are DISPLAY-ONLY fallbacks; GetSymbolPayouts() prefers the server's initData.paytable. count offset: arrayIndex + 2 (min 2 symbols) + reelCount (6) = on-screen count for ways games.
patterns.genericization: Standardize a paytable format: { symbolId: int[] } where value/SCALE = bet multiplier and array index maps to symbol-count via a documented offset. Provide an encode (designer table -> RLE int[]) and decode (int[] -> human ranges) utility pair in scripts/.

patterns.name: Reel strips as arrays of numeric symbol IDs
patterns.summary: Reel strips ('wheels'/'coinReels'/'reelStrips') are 2D arrays of integer symbol IDs, one inner array per reel column (e.g. to-the-top coinReels = 5 strips of 75 IDs each). Server may send them in config.wheels; otherwise a local fallback strip lives in config (StartingReels uses NAMES; coinReels/testReels use IDs). piggy-bank de-stacks oversized (multi-row) symbols from strips so a 1x3 symbol occupies one strip slot.
patterns.genericization: Define a canonical reel-strip JSON shape: int[][] keyed by mode (base/freegame/bonus). Provide helpers to map ID<->name via a symbol table, compute per-reel symbol frequency, and expand/collapse oversized stacked symbols.

patterns.name: Symbol ID table with type flags and drop weights
patterns.summary: A central symbol table maps integer ID -> {name, isWild, isBonus/scatter, isHigh/isLow, dropWeight}. Names use lp-NN (low pay), hp-NN (high pay), wild, scatter, superscatter. dropWeight is a PHYSICS/visual cascade weight (e.g. scatter 1.8, hp 1.7, lp 1.5) NOT a math probability — actual symbol probability lives in the server reel strips. Note an intentional ID collision (silver-wild & superscatter share 12) because reverse lookup is name->ID one-way and wilds are never sent as winSymbol.
patterns.genericization: Skill artifact: a symbol-definition schema (id, name, kind, isWild, isScatter, payoutRowRef, dropWeight). Separate 'visual drop weight' from 'math weight' explicitly to avoid confusion.

patterns.name: Cluster-pay + per-cell doubling multiplier grid
patterns.summary: 7x7 grid; any symbol type in 5+ orthogonally-adjacent positions forms a winning cluster. A multiplier grid tracks every cell: when a cell participates in a win it activates and doubles each subsequent cascade win (1->2->4->...->1024 cap); only cells >=2x contribute. Grid resets per round (base) but PERSISTS across all free-spin cascades. Server sends multiplierView/updatedMultiplierView as per-reel JSON strings of ints (-1 = inactive). winInfo.winningArray entries carry {symbol, pay, win, multiplierSum, clusterPositions}.
patterns.genericization: Add a math-mechanic 'cluster pays' module: flood-fill adjacency over a grid, min-cluster-size param, and an orthogonal 'persistent doubling multiplier grid' option. Encode multiplier grid as int[][] with sentinel for inactive cells.

patterns.name: Scatter 'machine' progression replacing probabilistic free-spin trigger
patterns.summary: Free-game trigger is a deterministic 10-level (or 7-level legacy) progression meter, not a flat scatter-count probability. Each landed scatter rolls a per-level weighted transition table (oneScatterTables/twoScatterTables, 18 tables = 9 levels x 2 cases, per mode baseGame/freegame/bonus/superbonus). 3+ scatters skip the table straight to max=trigger. Reward tiers map scatter count -> spins (e.g. retrigger reward [0,10,10,10,12,15,20,30], cap 100). Scatters persist on-screen across cascades. 'Boost' mode raises trigger odds and bet by 25-50%.
patterns.genericization: Math-feature skill: 'progression-meter trigger' with config = {levels, perLevelWeightedTransitionTables, straightToMaxThreshold, rewardByScatterCount, maxCap, persistAcrossCascades, boostMultiplier}. Emit ScatterLevelUp events {newScatters, prevLevel, newLevel, levelUps[], triggered}.

patterns.name: Cascade/tumble wave pipeline with intermediate/gravity views
patterns.summary: Each spin returns an ordered list of waves. Per wave the server sends gameView (current grid), winInfo, intermediateView (grid after winning symbols removed), gravityView (grid after remaining symbols fall + new symbols added), and updatedMultiplierView. Client diffs intermediateView vs gameView to find removals and gravityView vs intermediate to find additions, enabling fully data-driven cascade animation. Views are delivered as per-reel JSON STRINGS ('[3,4,9,...]') and must be JSON.parse-normalized first.
patterns.genericization: Define a 'cascade wave' record {gameView, removals, additions, winInfo, multiplierView, scatterLevelUps?} and a generic wave-processor that derives removal/addition indices by diffing successive views. Include a response-normalizer that JSON.parses stringified view arrays at the boundary (matches user's input-validation-at-boundaries rule).

patterns.name: Deterministic QA via RNG-seed cheat scenarios
patterns.summary: game/config/cheats.json (and server-md/*Cheats.json) holds named scenarios as 'rng: <comma-separated uint32 seeds>' strings, grouped into categories (baseGame, rushFeature, freeGame...). These seeds are replayed into the SERVER RNG (connector.play(action,bet,null,rng)) to force exact deterministic outcomes (Small/Big/Mega/Epic Win, max multiplier, feature triggers, N-cascade chains). This is the QA harness for math/feature outcomes — there is NO client-side Monte-Carlo simulator anywhere.
patterns.genericization: Skill artifact: a cheats/scenario schema {categories:[{id,name,cheats:[{id,name,cheat:'rng: ...'}]}]} plus a runner contract play(action,bet,rng). Pairs with a math-engine that accepts an injectable RNG seed for reproducible certification runs.

patterns.name: Win-threshold celebration tiers driven by win/bet multiple
patterns.summary: config.winThresholds is an ordered list {name, threshold (xBet), isBigWin/isMegaWin/isEpicWin, loopDur, loopFrames}. Client picks the highest tier where totalWin >= threshold*bet to choose the celebration. Purely presentational mapping of the server's numeric win to a band; identical structure across every game.
patterns.genericization: Generic 'win band' config (threshold in bet-multiples -> celebration tier). Reusable across any slot regardless of math model.

patterns.name: Buy-bonus / boost / bet-action model
patterns.summary: Spin actions are named ('main','boosted','fg','superfg') sourced from config.buyFeatures[].code; each maps to a bet calc (baseBet, baseBet*scale, baseBet*100, baseBet*500) and its own RTP value. Buy-bonus prices and per-mode rtpValues come from server config (config.fgBuy, config.rtpValues, config.cap.maxWin/freeGameMaxRound). maxWin caps (e.g. 25000xBet, 30000) terminate the round.
patterns.genericization: Math-config skill: per-mode parameter set {action, betMultiplier, targetRTP, buyPrice, maxWinCap, maxFreeSpins}. Standardize rtpValues as a per-action map the back office sets and the client only displays (rounded).

configFormats: config-desktop.json / config-mobile.json: top-level game math/layout config — reels, rows, maxWin, StartingReels (NAME[][]), winThresholds[], buyFeatures[] (title/price/rtp/code), reelsConfig (spin curves), zIndex[]. Cluster game adds reels:7/rows:7.
Reel strips: numeric int[][] one array per reel — server config.wheels.base / config.coinReels / config.testReels. IDs map to names via a symbol table.

paytable-{landscape,portrait,social-*}.json: DISPLAY-ONLY paytable; root {version:'2.0', pages:[{id,title(@-ref),gridColumns,elements:[...]}]}; element types include symbolTable/symbolAccordion (SymbolDef.payouts=[{range:'13-14',value:8}]), multiplierGrid, imageGroup, text/image. Authored by a standalone browser paytable-editor (export {version,pages}).

Server play response: {balance, wager:{data:{baseGame:{won,baseGameWaves:[{wave:{gameView,winInfo:{winning,multiplier,winningArray:[{symbol,pay,win,multiplierSum,clusterPositions}],intermediateView,gravityView,updatedMultiplierView},scatterLevelUps}}],baseGameEndView,scatterLevel,scatterActivated}, freeGame?:{initNumOfRounds,roundDetails:[{roundWaves,roundEndView,scatterLevel,retriggerRounds}],totalRounds}, totalWin}}}. View arrays delivered as JSON STRINGS per reel (e.g. '[3,4,9,1,5,4,4]').

Server info/config response: config.{bets(per action: available[]+default), fgBuy, cap{maxWin,freeGameMaxRound}, feature{per-mode oneScatterTables/twoScatterTables, wildMultiplierValues, reward tables}, rtpValues(per action %), initGameViews(per mode), wheels(reel strips)}.
cheats.json: {version, categories:[{id,name,cheats:[{id,name,cheat:'rng: <uint32 csv>'}]}]} — deterministic QA seed scenarios (note: one variant is UTF-16/BOM encoded).
scatter machine tables: config.feature[mode].{oneScatterTables, twoScatterTables} = weighted per-level transition tables; reward arrays e.g. freeGameRetriggerReward=[0,10,10,10,12,15,20,30].

TypeScript typed contracts (piggy-bank): ParsedGameConfig{reelStrips:Symbols[][], multiplierValues:number[], payouts:Record<Symbol,number[]>, fgBuy}; SpinData/LineWin/CascadeSymbolPosition/CollectorData/MultiplierData interfaces in ResponseParser.ts.
reusableArtifacts: RLE paytable codec: encode designer payout-table -> server int[] (value*10) and decode int[] -> human count-ranges ('8','13-14','19+'); port the algorithm from medusa GetSymbolPayouts (docs/architecture/14-paytable-data.md:408-468) and piggy-bank GameConfigParser._parsePayouts.
Reel-strip utilities: ID<->name mapping, per-reel symbol-frequency counter, oversized/stacked-symbol de-stacking (piggy-bank GameConfigParser._parseReelStrips:36).

response-normalizer: JSON.parse stringified per-reel view arrays at the boundary; VIEW_KEYS=['gameView','intermediateView','gravityView','multiplierView','updatedMultiplierView'] (sweety-treaty-cluster-pay/game/src/mechanics/response-normalizer.js + docs 09:122-137).
wave-processor / cascade diff: derive removal & addition indices by diffing gameView->intermediateView->gravityView (docs 09:208-223).

cluster mechanic: orthogonal flood-fill adjacency (min cluster size) + persistent doubling multiplier grid (encode as int[][] with -1 sentinel); spec in game_rules updated.txt + 09-server-protocol.md.
progression-meter (scatter machine) engine: weighted per-level transition tables + straight-to-max threshold + reward-by-count table + ScatterLevelUp event emitter (server-md/scatterMachine.md).
cheats/scenario schema + RNG-seed-injectable spin runner for deterministic certification (cheats.json format + connector.play(action,bet,null,rng) signature).
win-band/threshold config + selector (config winThresholds + win-parser.parseThreshold).
Per-mode math-parameter table {action,betMultiplier,targetRTP,buyPrice,maxWinCap,maxFreeSpins} from config.buyFeatures + server config.rtpValues/cap.
The medusa docs/architecture/*.md and sweety-treaty-cluster-pay docs/technical-reference/*.md are themselves portable reference templates for documenting a slot's math+protocol.
mapsToSkills: slot-math-designer (already present): feed it the RLE paytable codec, reel-strip + symbol-table schema, per-mode RTP/maxWin/buy-bonus parameter model, and the win-threshold band config.
math-config (proposed): canonical JSON schemas for reels(int[][]) / paytable(RLE int[]) / symbol-table / scatter-machine tables / per-action RTP+caps.
math-engine / math-mechanics (proposed): ways-pays, cluster-pays (flood-fill + persistent doubling multiplier grid), cascade/tumble wave generation, progression-meter free-spin trigger.

math-simulation / math-certification (proposed — GAP, nothing exists client-side): build a Monte-Carlo RTP/volatility/hit-rate/max-win simulator plus the RNG-seed cheat-scenario harness (the repos only have deterministic QA seeds, no statistical sim).
math-protocol (proposed): the spin-result + config-info JSON contract, response-normalizer (stringified views), and replay/recovery model.
gaps: No client-side RTP computation or Monte-Carlo/statistical simulation exists in ANY project — RTP, volatility, hit-rate and reel-weight tuning all happen on the unavailable remote math server. wagerforge will need to BUILD the simulator/certification tooling from scratch; these repos give only the I/O contract, not the math engine.

The actual reel-symbol weights / probability tables and the scatter-machine transition tables are server-side; only the consumed shape is visible. config.feature[mode].oneScatterTables values themselves are not in the client repos.

The connector (slotify/connector/connector.js) is a third-party MINIFIED bundle (~1.2MB) — the real server math API (endpoints, exact request signing, RNG algorithm) is opaque; only the documented method surface (play/info/authenticate/replay) is recoverable.

Target RTP is a placeholder in client config (buyFeatures[].rtp = 'XX.XX', game_rules RTP = '{main}%' template); real RTP arrives at runtime in server config.rtpValues. No game declares a concrete designed RTP number in-repo.

Two different code generations coexist (legacy CommonJS reskin lineage: zeus/medusa/tartarus vs TypeScript Slot Framework V2: piggy-bank/fruit-nudge) with different parser shapes — a generic skill must abstract over both rather than copy one.
Cluster game cheats file (sweetyClusterCheats.json) is UTF-16/BOM encoded — any porting tool must handle encoding detection at the boundary.
Paytable JSON files are display-only and can drift from server truth (the docs explicitly note social-portrait payout ranges differ); they are NOT a reliable source for the actual math paytable.

patterns.name: Two-tier framework/client separation (reskin-friendly engine core)
patterns.summary: Every game splits a reusable engine from per-title game code. Family A: top-level framework/src (base-gamestate, resource-manager, base-layer, ui-buttons, menus, info-bars, widgets) vs game/src (title-specific reels, effects, layers). Family B: src/framework (managers, sequences, factories, events, ui) vs src/client (config, factories, scene, sequences, menus). CLAUDE.md files explicitly state 'framework code reusable across projects, game/client code title-specific'. Medusa is a literal reskin of Tartarus; timings.json and many framework files are byte-identical across titles.
patterns.genericization: Codify a generic 'slot engine vs game-skin' boundary as a build-* scaffolding skill: an engine package (state/sequence runtime, managers, reel/symbol primitives, UI widgets) + a thin per-title layer (config JSON, symbol map, scene JSON, asset bundles, title sequences). New title = copy skin layer + swap assets/config, never touch engine.

patterns.name: Config-driven reel-strip + win-threshold math contract
patterns.summary: Game math lives in JSON, not code: StartingReels as 2D arrays of symbol-id strings, winThresholds as ordered list keyed on win-as-multiple-of-bet (threshold * betSize) with celebration tier flags (isBigWin/isMegaWin/isEpicWin/isBoring/noCelebration) and per-tier loop duration/frame counts. WinParser.parseThreshold iterates thresholds high-to-low and returns the first tier whose threshold*bet is met.
patterns.genericization: Define a generic reel-strip + win-tier schema (Zod/JSON Schema): reels/rows, reelStrips[][], winTiers[{name, betMultipleThreshold, flags, loopMs, loopFrames}]. Feeds a slot-math/config skill; the FE win-presentation tier selection is pure data lookup and is portable across any payline or cluster game.

patterns.name: Bezier-curve reel-spin physics with turbo/normal profiles
patterns.summary: Reel start/stop motion is driven by named cubic-bezier control-point arrays in config (startCurve, stopCurve, hotreelStopCurve, bonusStopCurve, quickStopCurve, stopCurveBump) plus separate turboSpin/normalSpin tables of cascade gaps and durations. A 3D trapezoid PIXI.Filter (custom GLSL fragment shader) gives reels perspective and an oscillating rotationX tilt, orientation-aware (portrait vs landscape ratios).
patterns.genericization: Extract a reusable 'reel motion profile' config (named bezier curves + turbo/normal timing tables) and an optional perspective-reel WebGL filter as a drop-in. Feeds a build-reels / reel-spin-animation skill. Note the perf pattern: ticker only recomputes uniforms on size/orientation change.

patterns.name: Sequence/Step coroutine engine (Family B) vs Gamestate state-machine + Timeline (Family A)
patterns.summary: Two distinct but parallel orchestration models for spin/win choreography. Family B: an abstract Sequence<TParams> composed of Steps with blocking/concurrent/race/skip-group semantics, GC-free via StepFrame object pooling, GSAP tweens inside leaf steps (EaseReelVelocityStep, CountUpStep). Skippable steps power turbo/skip. Family A: explicit GamestateManager FSM (start/loop/stop-spinning, start/process-cascade, evaluate-result, idle, notification, menu) extending BaseGamestate, with a custom Timeline that schedules effects at start offsets.
patterns.genericization: Offer BOTH choreography templates in a build-game-flow skill: (1) a pooled Sequence/Step library (DelayStep, CallbackStep, ParallelStep, ConditionalStep, LoopStep, PromiseStep, SoundEventStep, RaceConcurrentStep, SkipGroupStep) for skippable async flows; (2) a slot FSM template with the canonical states. Both wrap GSAP/tween + sound hooks at the leaf. Recommend Sequence/Step as the modern default.

patterns.name: Symbol Matrix object pooling: sprite-default, spine-on-animate
patterns.summary: Symbols are pooled MatrixSymbol objects that render as cheap sprites at rest and swap to a Spine (from a symbol-type-specific SpinePool) only while animating, then swap back. No runtime object creation (created at game start; only MenuManager creates/destroys at runtime). Oversized/stacked symbols use shared-reference cells (multiple grid positions point to one object). SymbolConfig carries spine/skin/staticTexture/blurredTexture/animations/anchor/value-font.
patterns.genericization: Package a generic SymbolMatrix + PixiObjectPool + SpinePool as a build-symbols skill. Core rule: 'sprites at rest, spines only mid-animation, return to sprite after'; pool by generic MatrixSymbol not per-type; shared-reference cells for stacked/oversized symbols. Drives the <200MB / 60fps perf targets in CLAUDE.md.

patterns.name: Data-driven responsive scene graph (JSON tree + objects with portrait/landscape blocks)
patterns.summary: UI/layout is authored as JSON: a 'tree' of nested node names plus an 'objects' map giving each node a type (Sprite/BitmapText/Button/Container/Spine), asset binding, anchors, boundary, and per-orientation override blocks (portrait{}/landscape{}). SceneManager builds Pixi display objects from this and re-lays-out on resize; getNode gives injected access. Family A parallels this with layout-game-desktop/mobile.json and a layer zIndex array in config.
patterns.genericization: Define a generic scene-JSON schema + SceneManager (tree + typed objects + orientation override blocks + named z-layer ordering) for a build-ui / build-scene skill. Lets non-engineers reposition UI per device without code; portrait/landscape switching is first-class.

patterns.name: Win-presentation tiering and count-up choreography
patterns.summary: Win presentation is a layered, tier-driven flow: a dedicated win-presentation layer/sequence picks actions (PRESENT_WIN / PRESENT_BIG_WIN / PRESENT_MAX_WIN / ADD_FREESPIN / FREESPIN_CAP_REACHED) from the matched win tier, plays big-win symbol anims + win-line show/hide + reel-bg-win + count-up. Count-up is a GSAP tween over a numeric field formatted by an injected TextFormatter (currency/grouping/round). Win lines have full lifecycle steps (Init/Show/Hide/Clear, CycleWins loop).
patterns.genericization: Ship a build-win-presentation skill: tier->presentation action map, win-line lifecycle steps, big-win celebration sequence (mask symbols, fade UI, loop animation by tier loopMs/loopFrames), and a reusable currency count-up step. Wire tier selection to the win-threshold config schema above.

patterns.name: Multi-device / multi-operator build matrix from one source
patterns.summary: A single source builds desktop/mobile/responsive x debug/prod x real-money/social via env-cmd .env files and an npm-run-all pipeline (build-css -> build-sprites -> bundle -> terser -> copy-dist). Asset trees mirror this (assets/desktop, assets/mobile, assets/social-desktop, assets/social-mobile; manifest-desktop/mobile/social-desktop/social-mobile.json). Device detection picks the manifest/config at runtime.
patterns.genericization: Template the build matrix (device x environment x money-mode) and the parallel asset-tree + per-variant manifest convention into a build-pipeline / scaffolding skill. Family A uses Browserify+Babel+Terser; Family B uses Webpack+TS+ts-loader — offer both, prefer the TS/Webpack one.

patterns.name: Operator/platform connector ('slotify') decoupling RGS integration
patterns.summary: Nearly every title (12/13) ships a slotify/connector/connector.js shared layer that brokers balance, operator mode, and window.postMessage handshakes (spinStart with bet posted to parent frame; balance heavily referenced). Game code calls Utils.PostMessageToParent('spinStart', {bet}) etc., keeping platform glue out of game logic.
patterns.genericization: Abstract a generic 'RGS/operator connector' module (balance, bet, spin lifecycle postMessage protocol, social vs real-money mode) as a build-platform-integration skill — the seam that swaps casino operators without touching game/engine code.

patterns.name: Spine-first animation with sprite-atlas fallback and asset-build automation
patterns.summary: Both families standardize on pixi-spine 4.x for complex anims (bg, character, big_win, scatter, wild, transitions, logo) organized one-folder-per-animation under anims/ with the json+atlas+webp triad; static UI/symbols use free-tex-packer-packed spritesheets with WebP (+PNG fallback) and bitmap fonts (.fnt). A bespoke build-sprites pipeline (free-tex-packer-core + Jimp + direct cwebp binary) plus spine utility scripts (extract-spine-static, recolor-spine-anim, scale-mobile-spine, shrink-mobile-spine-atlas) automate asset prep with mtime-based incremental skipping and bounded concurrency.
patterns.genericization: Codify the asset convention (Spine triad per anim folder; packed WebP atlas + bitmap fonts for static; PNG fallback) and port the build-sprites + spine-utility scripts into wagerforge scripts/ for an asset-pipeline / asset-audit skill. Naming: lp-/hp- prefixes (Family A) and L0-L4/H5-H9 (Family B) for low/high-pay tiers.

area: Provably-Fair / RNG patterns in crypto mini-game frontends

patterns.name: Commit-reveal seed lifecycle over a thin client (GDK fairness layer)
patterns.summary: Every game shares a GDK fairness stack. The backend owns the seed lifecycle; the client only displays commitments and triggers rotation. /fairness/activeRngSeeds returns {clientSeed, serverSeedHash, nextServerSeedHash, nonce, unfinishedGames} (responseTypes.ts:104). The serverSeedHash is the COMMIT (SHA-256 of the active server seed); nextServerSeedHash pre-commits the next seed before it is used. The plaintext active server seed is only revealed AFTER rotation (so past rounds become verifiable but the in-use seed stays hidden). Rotation = POST /fairness/updateClientSeed {clientSeed} which atomically reveals the old server seed, activates nextServerSeed, generates a new nextServerSeedHash, and resets nonce to 0 (connector.ts:271, server.ts:237). Per-round commitment snapshot available via /fairness/roundRngState (connector.ts:263).
patterns.genericization: Define a FairSeedState type {clientSeed, serverSeedHash, nextServerSeedHash, nonce, status, unfinishedGames} and a SeedRepository interface (getActiveSeeds, rotate(newClientSeed)->revealsOldServerSeed, getRoundState(roundId)). Make commit=sha256(serverSeed) and the double-commit (current+next) the canonical contract. Strip the axios/redux specifics.

patterns.name: Client-side HMAC-SHA256 verifier with explicit float derivation (the reference verifier)
patterns.summary: ChaseServerProxy.computeU is the ONLY real client-side provably-fair derivation in the whole corpus and is directly portable. It uses Web Crypto (crypto.subtle), no third-party dep: importKey(serverSeed as HMAC/SHA-256 key) then sign(message). Message format is `${clientSeed}:${nonce}`. Bytes -> hex -> take first 13 hex chars (52 bits) -> parseInt(hex,16) -> divide by 2**52 to get u in [0,1). That u then feeds the crash-point inverse-CDF rollCrashPoint(u): if u<houseEdge return 1.0 else floor(min((1-h)/(1-u), maxCrash)*100)/100 (chaseMath.ts:71). This is a clean, testable seed->uniform->outcome pipeline that mirrors what the server runs, used as an offline fallback when the platform proveFairness endpoint doesn't yet know the game.
patterns.genericization: Port computeU verbatim as fair-rng core: hmacSha256ToFloat(serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, {bits=52, cursor=0}). Generalize the 13-hex/2**52 slice into a configurable bit-width and a byte cursor so games needing multiple floats can advance through the 32-byte digest (and re-hash with an incrementing counter when exhausted, the standard Stake-style scheme). Keep message format pluggable (':'-joined vs '-' joined) since it is a wire contract.

patterns.name: randomizations[] array as the seed->outcome bridge (verification re-render)
patterns.summary: VerifyFairnessResponse = {hashes:{hex,bytes[]}, randomizations: RandomizationsData[]} where each randomization = {limit, extractions[], randomNumber, gameEvent} (responseTypes.ts:163). Each array element is ONE independent RNG draw for the round; gameEvent carries the decoded per-game outcome (roll, result, multiplierIndex, gems[], crashPointMultiplier, cards, bucketIndex/bucketMultiplier, rolls[], flips[], hands[]...). Per-game Result components are pure presenters: they index into randomizations and render gameEvent fields, trusting the server's authoritative decode (LimboResult reads randomizations[last].gameEvent.result; PlinkoResult documents randomizations[0]=bucket roll, randomizations[1]=ball roll for the two-axis Insane tier). extractions[] {cursor,hashIndex,offset,integer} exposes exactly which digest bytes produced the number, enabling byte-level audit.
patterns.genericization: Standardize a Randomization{floatValue, extractions:[{cursor,hashIndex,offset,integer}], gameEvent} and a VerificationResult{commitments, randomizations[]}. The mapping 'N independent draws -> N randomizations' (one per peg-set / card / roll) is the reusable contract; per-game decoders consume floats[] and emit a typed gameEvent. Provide reference decoders for the common shapes: uniform-multiplier (limbo/crash inverse-CDF), Fisher-Yates shuffle (mines/cards/keno), and binomial-bucket (plinko).

patterns.name: Hash-chain games use a separate verifyHashFairness proof path
patterns.summary: Crash/Aviator-family ('crash0','aviator','crash1','aviator0') are routed to gateway.verifyHashFairness(game, hash, seed, hashIndex) instead of verifyFairness(game, serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce) (ResultLoader.tsx:186, gateway.ts:241, connector.ts:321). These are pre-generated hash-chain games: the operator commits to a terminal hash and each round's outcome derives from hash_i where hashIndex walks backward down the chain (each hash is sha256 of the next), with the player's clientSeed as the only mixed-in salt ('seed'). The Fairness UI swaps its labels to seed/hash instead of clientSeed/serverSeed for these games.
patterns.genericization: Model two distinct fair-proof modes: (1) per-round HMAC(serverSeed; clientSeed, nonce) [reseeding/commit-reveal] and (2) hash-chain (commit terminal hash; outcome_i = f(hash_i, sharedSeed); verify hash_{i} -> sha256 -> hash_{i-1}). A generic verifier should expose both and pick by game class. Crash-style RTP math (inverse-CDF survival P(crash>x)=(1-h)/x) is shared across both modes.

patterns.name: Live-tunable RTP/house-edge config decoupled from the fairness derivation
patterns.summary: chaseMath keeps the math pure and deterministic (caller supplies u) while all economic parameters (targetRTP, houseEdge, maxCrash, tick pacing) are LIVE getters hydrated from a /game/info config object (chaseServerConfig) so operators can retune RTP without a client redeploy, and defaults (DEFAULT_CHASE_CONFIG, RTP 0.98 / edge 0.02 / maxCrash 100000) drive offline/unit-test behaviour. RTP is also passed per-game in the verify request payload (ResultLoader builds data.rtp=0.98 for limbo/slide, 1.0 for the '...0' zero-edge variants, data.variant='rtp98'/'rtp97' for blitz/drill/packs).
patterns.genericization: Separate FairOutcome math (pure: takes u + economic params) from EconomyConfig (RTP/edge/caps, server-hydrated with safe defaults). The 'zero-edge variant' convention (game vs game+'0' with rtp=1) and 'variant=rtpNN' string are a reusable way to express multiple RTP SKUs of one game under one verifier.

mapsToSkills: fair-rng-core (HMAC-SHA256 seed->float derivation, configurable bit-width/cursor, 52-bit & byte-cursor schemes)
fair-commit-reveal (server-seed hashing/commit, double-commit next-seed, reveal-on-rotation, nonce increment, seed lifecycle repository)
fair-verify (player-side verifier: re-derive floats from revealed seeds, decode to gameEvent, diff against settled result; supports both per-round-HMAC and hash-chain modes)
fair-outcome-mappers (uniform inverse-CDF for crash/limbo, Fisher-Yates for mines/cards/keno, binomial-bucket for plinko; one-randomization-per-draw contract)
fair-economy-config (server-hydrated RTP/house-edge/caps with safe defaults, zero-edge & rtpNN variant SKUs)
gaps: MODULO BIAS NOT HANDLED IN-REPO: the only client derivation (computeU) takes 52 bits and divides by 2**52 to get a float, then crash math uses inverse-CDF (no integer modulo), so no bias there. BUT for discrete-outcome games (cards 0..51, mines tiles, keno) the integer reduction happens SERVER-SIDE and is never shown client-side — there is no client code proving the server avoids modulo bias (e.g. rejection sampling). A generic fair-verify skill must re-implement the reduction itself (rejection sampling / Stake-style 4-byte-float scaling) rather than trusting the server's gameEvent, otherwise 'verification' is circular.

NON-CRYPTOGRAPHIC CLIENT SEED: generateSeedString() uses Math.random() over a 62-char alphabet, default length 8 (utils/index.ts:5). ~48 bits of NON-CSPRNG entropy for the player's seed. Should use crypto.getRandomValues and a longer length; flag in fair-commit-reveal as a required-fix pattern.

VERIFICATION IS DELEGATED, NOT INDEPENDENT: for every game except chase, the 'verify' button calls the operator's own /game/proveFairness endpoint and the UI simply re-renders whatever gameEvent the SERVER returns (LimboResult/PlinkoResult just display server values). This is trust-the-house, not provably-fair — a true verifier must recompute outcomes locally from the revealed serverSeed+clientSeed+nonce. ChaseServerProxy is the only one that does. This is the single biggest gap to close in the wagerforge skill.

STUBBED HASHES IN DUMMY SERVERS: offline/dummy servers return serverSeedHash:'0'.repeat(64) and verifyFairness:{randomizations:[]} (HexshotDummyServer.ts:191), and Sling's generateRound uses Math.random as rng (HexshotDummy.ts:63). Fine for offline play but means no fairness coverage in the local path; a reference impl should wire the real HMAC derivation into the dummy so offline == verifiable.

NO TEST VECTORS / KNOWN-ANSWER TESTS for the HMAC derivation: no committed (serverSeed,clientSeed,nonce)->u fixtures were found for computeU, so the byte-slice + 2**52 convention is unverified against a reference (e.g. Stake's published vectors). A ported fair-rng-core should ship KATs.

MESSAGE-FORMAT AMBIGUITY: chase uses `${clientSeed}:${nonce}` but discrete games' server reduction order (which byte cursor, big/little-endian, hashIndex semantics in extractions{cursor,hashIndex,offset}) is implied by the response but never executed client-side, so the exact wire contract for multi-float games is undocumented in this repo and must be reverse-engineered or specified before generic verification is sound.

area: House-edge math + game-server (bet/settle) patterns extracted from MetaWin crypto mini-games (standalone "GDK" generation + monorepo "studio" generation) for a generic iGaming skills library
projectsExamined: mini-game-chase (GDK, crash + parallelStreaks ladder, source-cited chaseMath.ts)
mini-games-monorepo (studio: packages/studio, mw-number-flow, translations; apps/* Vite games; RTP-variant build system)

patterns.name: Constant-house-edge crash/target math (RTP-flat EV)
patterns.summary: For continuous-probability games (Limbo, Dice, Chase), payout and win-chance are linked so EV is constant regardless of player target: winChance% = (1-houseEdge)/multiplier*100; multiplier = (1-houseEdge)/(winChance/100). Crash uses survival law P(crashPoint > x) = (1-h)/x with an 'instabust' atom at x=1.0 of probability exactly h, giving EV(x) = [(1-h)/x]*x*bet = (1-h)*bet for ALL x. Chase proves it also holds for N parallel geometric rungs each staking bet/N.
patterns.genericization: Skill primitive 'constant-edge-curve': given houseEdge h, expose multiplier<->winChance bijection and a rollCrashPoint(u) = u<h ? 1.0 : min((1-h)/(1-u), maxCrash). Parameterize h, min/max multiplier, decimal rounding (floor to 2dp). Provide an EV-invariance assertion generator. Strip MetaWin specifics (tequity/metawin providers, $ caps).

patterns.name: Binomial Plinko buckets with EV-calibrated multiplier tables
patterns.summary: Bucket k of rows pegs has P = C(rows,k)/2^rows (fair 50/50 per peg). Per-(rows,tier) multiplier arrays are hand-calibrated so Sum_k P(k)*mult[k] = RTP (0.98). rollBucket(u) walks the cumulative binomial CDF until u<cumulative. The 100k variant adds an independent second axis (ball multiplier) drawn from weights {1:8000, 5:1400, 10:500, 100:100}/10000 with E[ball]=3.000 exactly, so E[total]=E[bucket]*E[ball]=(0.98/3)*3=0.98 — branding a 100,000x jackpot while holding RTP. Source code matches docs exactly.
patterns.genericization: Skill 'plinko-table-designer': inputs rows range + tier RTP target, outputs symmetric multiplier arrays calibrated to EV target, plus rollBucket CDF sampler and an optional independent multiplier-axis (weighted reel) calibrated so product of expectations = RTP. Generic 'two-axis EV decomposition' is reusable for any bucket*reel combo game.

patterns.name: Server-authoritative outcome + cosmetic deterministic physics replay
patterns.summary: All RNG is server-side; client never generates outcomes. Plinko runs a real Matter.js fixed-timestep simulation but seeds the launch-x from a pre-baked pool and then OVERWRITES the physics-derived landing bucket with the server's multiplierIndex. 'Client physics != client outcome'. physicsPattern.ts is ~456KB of {x,y:-4} launch points (not trajectories); determinism comes from fixed dt + static pegs, not keyframe replay.
patterns.genericization: Skill rule 'visual-sim-must-not-decide-outcome': any physics/animation layer is cosmetic; the settled result is always the server field. Provide a dev-only assert that flags drift between simulated landing and server index. Generic to wheel, roulette, darts, ball-drop.

patterns.name: Round-lock / roundId multi-step bet-settle protocol with idempotent recovery
patterns.summary: Multi-step games (Mines, Dragon Tower, Pepe) keep one round open across many REST calls. First action ('main') sends bet and NO roundId; server returns roundId; all subsequent 'continue'/'takeWin' actions send roundId and bet=undefined. wager.next array ([], ['continue'], ['continue','takeWin']) drives which buttons are enabled and whether the round is live. doComplete flag decides whether /game/complete is inline or separate. On boot, /game/recover replays/settles any open round; HAZARD: recover() must return null/204 (a truthy {} triggers a spurious round). Win-cap detected purely by finalWin !== wager.win.
patterns.genericization: Skill 'stateful-round-engine': model rounds as {roundId, openActions[], settled}. Codify invariants: bet only on first action, roundId locks subsequent calls, idempotent recover (null-for-none), explicit settle. Provide a server-contract test matrix (continue/takeWin/complete/recover). Generic to any cash-out-anytime survival game.

patterns.name: Commit-reveal HMAC-SHA256 provable fairness with per-game decoder
patterns.summary: One scheme across both generations: server commits serverSeedHash (SHA-256) before round; reveals raw seed only after the seed pair is rotated; rotation is BLOCKED while unfinishedGames.length>0. Outcome = HMAC-SHA256(serverSeed, clientSeed:nonce:cursor) byte stream chunked into 32-bit ints, normalized to float in [0,1), mapped per game. /game/proveFairness returns hashes[{hex,bytes[]}] + randomizations[{extractions[{cursor,hashIndex,offset,integer}], randomNumber, gameEvent}]. Client owns ONLY the per-game gameEvent decoder/renderer. Multiplayer/crash family uses {hash,hashIndex,seed} instead of {serverSeed,clientSeed,nonce}, gated by isMultiplayerGame().
patterns.genericization: Skill 'provable-fairness-kit': generic verifier that takes (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce) -> byte stream -> float -> per-game mapping function (injected). Ship a registry of decoders (limbo:result, mines:minesPositionsBuffer.slice(numMines) via Fisher-Yates, keno:gems, plinko:bucket+ball, diamonds:integer%7). Codify rotation-blocked-by-open-rounds and single-vs-multiplayer field sets.

patterns.name: Native-server / fiat-display dual-currency money invariant
patterns.summary: Server ALWAYS speaks native wallet currency (crypto or social coin); fiat (USD/EUR) is display-only and never a balance currency. Every bet converts display->native before POST (convertToNativeValue, floor-rounded); every response deep-walks money keys (/win|balance|wager|amount|bet/i) to fiat while preserving native under a parallel key (balance->cryptoBalance). Native precision = auth currencyDecimals; fiat precision = host displayFiatCurrencyDecimals — never hardcode per-currency decimals. Live FX pushed by host via getFiatCurrencyRates; game never fetches rates. Freebet/campaign bets are NEVER fiat-converted (always native).
patterns.genericization: Skill 'dual-currency-money': always-native settlement + display overlay; floor rounding so players are never over-credited; parallel native key preservation; server-driven precision; host-pushed FX. Studio's mathOperations (decimal.js builder) is the float-safe money path. WARNING to port: roundDown() uses ROUND_CEIL / roundUp() uses ROUND_UP (naming inverted) — fix on port.

patterns.name: Inline window.mwgame host bridge (no iframe/postMessage)
patterns.summary: Game mounts INLINE into host's <div id="mw-mini-game"> via createRoot, sharing host window. Contract = global window.mwgame object (installed once, guarded) + a private EventTarget. Host->game: unmountReactApp, updateConfigData (data-* + setOverride), refreshGameState (full teardown+re-auth on currency switch), updateUserToken, updateClientSeed/getActiveRngSeeds/roundRngState, getFiatCurrencyRates. Game->host CustomEvents: balanceUpdated (always native), gameSessionUpdated ({inSession} = game owns balance this round), bet/win telemetry. Config resolved URL-query-first then data-attribute. Session must ALWAYS be released — every play onError calls endGameSession().
patterns.genericization: Skill 'embedded-game-host-bridge': a transport-agnostic bridge interface (mount/unmount/refresh/token/seeds/fx/session) with an internal event bus; bracket every round with startSession/endSession; always release session on error. Generic to any embeddable game widget. Note WebSocket outlier (Aviator/Snake) replaces per-round REST with a WS session that IS the session.

patterns.name: Lottery paytable lookup with hypergeometric probability + risk tiers
patterns.summary: Keno: win = wonMultiplier*bet where wonMultiplier = multipliers[risk][pickedCount][matchCount]. Risk tier (Classic/Low/Medium/High) reshapes the payout curve flat->high-variance; per-(risk,picks) RTP ~98.75-99.00% (avg 98.93%). Exact match probability is hypergeometric P(X=k)=C(p,k)*C(40-p,10-k)/C(40,10); server also ships multipliersProbabilities[picks][match] for tooltips. Diamonds uses the analogous gem-frequency 'poker hand' partition paytable ([5]=five-of-a-kind etc.) with explicit counts/probabilities and EV-contribution per row; Blitz uses birthday-paradox P(N)=prod_{i=0}^{N-1}(52-i)/52, mult=RTP/P(N) giving exact EV=RTP-1.
patterns.genericization: Skill 'paytable-rtp-validator': given a paytable[tier][picks][matches] and the analytic distribution (hypergeometric / multinomial-partition / birthday-product), compute realized RTP per tier and assert within tolerance; flag tiers that drift. Generic to keno, diamonds, bars, blitz, any pick-and-match game.

patterns.name: Progressive-ladder multiplier with provably-fair per-row randomization
patterns.summary: Dragon Tower: multiplier(row) = RTP*(columns/eggs)^row; survival P = (eggs/columns)^row; EV when cashing at any row = RTP*bet (the (eggs/columns)^N and (columns/eggs)^N cancel). Difficulty = volatility dial via columns/eggs ratio (Easy 4/3 .. Master 4/1 -> 259,522x full clear). 27 server randomizations (9 rows x 3 Fisher-Yates) reconstruct the egg layout. Same EV-cancellation structure underlies Mines (hypergeometric per-pick survival).
patterns.genericization: Skill 'ladder-game-math': parameterize (rows, safe/total ratio per row, RTP); auto-derive per-row multiplier and survival tables and prove EV=RTP*bet at every cash-out point. Reusable for towers, mines, frog-crossing.

patterns.name: RTP-variant build system (one bundle, N RTP variants via env)
patterns.summary: Studio monorepo builds N RTP variants from ONE source bundle: rtp.config.json declares variants (gameCode/rtpPercentage/urlPath); vite.config reads RTP_VARIANT env, sets VITE_GAME_CODE + VITE_RTP_PERCENTAGE via define, rewrites the CDN path, outputs to build-rtp<pct>. The backend selects the RTP table purely from the reported gameCode; the client only DISPLAYS VITE_RTP_PERCENTAGE. Source bundles are byte-identical across variants. GDK equivalent: runtime alternate game codes (limbo0, dragon-tower0 rtp=1, Dice Zero=1.0).
patterns.genericization: Skill 'rtp-variant-pipeline': a config-driven build matrix that stamps gameCode+rtpPercentage into one bundle and fans out to per-variant CDN folders; backend keys RTP off gameCode. Generic deploy pattern for any house-edge-tunable game line.

gaps: Server-side outcome algorithms are NOT in the client for several games — the exact HMAC->crash-point distribution for Aviator, and the per-tier probability tables for Bars (config.tiles) cannot be verified from client source; need a network trace or server repo. The wagerforge math skills will need to re-derive/own the server side, not just port client decoders.

RTP is reported inconsistently within single games (Limbo UI 0.97 vs proveFairness fallback 0.98; Dragon Tower info.json 0.97 / README 0.99 / code 0.98; Pepe 96% vs 1% vs 0.99). Server /game/info config.rtp is authoritative — client fallbacks are unreliable as a math source. Treat docs' table RTPs as illustrative, server value as truth.

Win-cap units unverified: settings.winCap parsed as a string 'in smallest currency unit' but display fallbacks are USD ($500k/$250k); for non-USD wallets the hardcoded $ prefix may mislead. Cap detection relies on finalWin !== wager.win and silently misses caps where server returns finalWin===wager.win.
Keno gateway does NOT call updateGameSession(true/false) on bet start/end (unlike Limbo/Mines) — host session tracking gap on some operators; a generic engine must make session bracketing mandatory.

HEXSHOT (sling) ships a non-fair dummy: Math.random(), provablyFair:'false', verifier file does not exist — do NOT use as a fairness reference. Snake/Aviator use hash-based multiplayer fairness with no per-player seed.

asyncWinInPlay/asyncWinInComplete/doComplete semantics are under-specified across nearly every game (they change WHERE the win lands in the balance flow); Limbo even has a dead if/else where both branches are identical. Live per-operator config could not be confirmed from client code.

GDK is copy-pasted per repo and has DRIFTED (connector.ts/gateway.ts/strategyBetManager.ts differ between games; HTTP timeout 10s vs 30s in Plinkos; currency allow-lists disagree). Any ported pattern must be reconciled against the specific game, not assumed canonical.

Test coverage is thin: only Plinko-100k (math), Blitz, HEXSHOT, Chase, Snake have real game-specific tests; all studio apps and most GDK games have zero — the math models (Mines parser, Dragon Tower formula, Diamonds/Keno paytables) are high-value UNTESTED areas. wagerforge qa-* skills should fill these.

physicsPattern.ts has no CI guard — if physics constants change, recorded launch-x lands balls in wrong visual buckets, caught only by a dev-only assert. A generic cosmetic-sim skill needs a regression guard.

Multiplier tables for Keno/Diamonds are served live from server (infoResponse.config.multipliers) — the README/doc tables are snapshots that may be outdated; a verifier must compare a live /game/info against analytic distributions.

patterns.name: IGame contract — single play() dispatch as the RGS round entry point
patterns.summary: Every game is a module implementing a uniform interface: name, bets (per-action: available stake array, default, coin cost multiplier, maxWin, validate flag), play({bet, action, config}, random) -> {win, data}, config(variant) math selector, and stats collectors. The platform/RGS injects the RNG as the second arg of play(); the game is pure logic and never touches wallet/DB. play() switches on `action` (main, baseBoosted, feature-buy 'fg', 'elvis') and normalizes feature-buy bets by their coin cost before running math. This is the clean RGS<->game-logic boundary.
patterns.genericization: Generic 'game-server contract' skill: a GameModule interface { id, bets: Record<action,{stakes,default,coinCost,maxWin,validate}>, play(ctx, rng)->{win,data,state?,next?}, config(variant), stats }. RNG injected by host, never imported. Game logic is a pure function of (bet, action, config, rng). Feature-buy normalization (bet/coinCost) and per-action maxWin (cap/coinCost) are reusable rules. Abstract away Slotify/@slotify/gdk specifics.

patterns.name: Multi-step state machine for instant/crash games (deferred settlement)
patterns.summary: Instant games (flip, crash, cashout) return {win, state, data, next[]}. Convention: win=0 during intermediate steps so the host does NOT settle; state={} (empty) signals round concluded -> settle the bet; next[] tells client available actions ('continue','cashout'). Outcomes are pre-generated up front and stored in state under `_underscore`-prefixed keys that must never be serialized to the client. Autobet replays a predetermined pick array, stopping at first loss.
patterns.genericization: Reusable 'deferred-settlement state machine' pattern: settlement trigger = (win>0 || state empty); hidden server state via key-prefix convention; pre-generated outcome arrays; client-action whitelist via next[]. Maps directly to crash/instant game skills.

patterns.name: Provably-fair verification hook (proveFairness)
patterns.summary: Each instant game exposes proveFairness(addRandomization, data) calling addRandomization(range, rngValue => publicResult) once PER source of randomness, re-deriving the public outcome with the SAME formula the live game used. Lets players independently verify outcomes from the recorded RNG values.
patterns.genericization: Generic 'provably-fair' skill component: a verifier callback registered per RNG source that must reuse the exact production formula and declared range (2**32 for float, 2**N for N binary events). Drives fairness/transparency features independent of vendor.

patterns.name: RNG injection + TrackedRNG record/replay for reproducible rounds
patterns.summary: Host injects an integer RNG (randomInteger(limit) in [0,limit-1]); games adopt one convention (integer gameRNG(limit) OR float gameRNG(2**32)/2**32 in [0,1)). TrackedRNG wraps it to auto-record every draw with a 'purpose' label, supports load([..])/reset() replay, and mark() phase separators — enabling exact round reproduction (cheats/QA scenarios) with zero RTP difference vs prod (transparent passthrough). Guarded by logCheats flag so prod path is the raw RNG.
patterns.genericization: Reusable RNG abstraction: IntegerRng interface + a recording/replay decorator (sequence+purpose capture, deterministic replay). Port TrackedRNG.ts nearly verbatim into wagerforge scripts as a generic deterministic-replay RNG harness for QA/repro. Flag-gate dev-only behavior.

patterns.name: Numeric integrity rules: fixValue rounding, >= max-win caps, exclusive interval bounds
patterns.summary: Money math must round every accumulation (fixValue = round(x*100)/100) to avoid float drift; max-win caps use >= never == (float equality is unsafe); win-distribution buckets use exclusive upper bound [lo,hi) with a final Infinity bucket so no win is silently lost or double-counted. Feature-buy RTP must normalize the buy-in stake.
patterns.genericization: Generic money/RTP-integrity checklist for build-* and qa-* skills: round-on-accumulate helper, >=-cap rule, half-open interval buckets with Infinity terminator, stake normalization for feature-buys. These are vendor-agnostic correctness invariants.

patterns.name: Bet→settle round lifecycle: RGS → adapter → external wallet, correlation-tagged
patterns.summary: Production flow: client hits RGS /game/play; RGS runs the game module then hands the transaction to the adapter; adapter makes an outbound HTTP call to the operator's external wallet to withdraw the stake (bet) and later deposit the win (with asyncWin=true for the win leg). Every hop shares correlationId + sessionId + roundId. Routes are split by service: /game/*,/balance,/authenticate,/walletMessage->rgs; /wallet/*,/launch/*,/rgs/*->adapter; /fairness/*->rng. An incident showed the adapter's outbound wallet connection pool/per-player queue saturating, adding ~40s between receiving the bet and initiating the wallet HTTP call while the wallet itself responded in ~286ms — and the adapter's responseTime metric only measured the HTTP call, hiding the queue wait.
patterns.genericization: Generic RGS transaction lifecycle: RGS(game outcome) -> wallet-adapter(debit stake / credit win) -> operator wallet, with bet and win as separate transactions (async win), all carrying correlation/session/round IDs. Reusable failure-mode lesson: measure END-TO-END (handoff→wallet response) not just the HTTP call; bound outbound pool + per-player queue; crashed RGS can orphan in-flight transactions and leak pool connections. Feeds wallet-integration + reliability skills.

patterns.name: Path-based service routing + WAF/rate-limit edge for game endpoints
patterns.summary: Ingress maps URL paths to microservices (rgs/adapter/websocket/promo/rng/back-office). Cloud Armor edge: layer-7 DDoS adaptive protection, preconfigured OWASP rules (scanner detection, protocol-attack/SQLi/XSS), country-block via region_code header injection + deny, a deny(404) rule that rejects any path not in the known route map, and an IP-keyed throttle (deny 429) that EXEMPTS money/critical paths (/graphql,/feed,/wallet,/rgs) from rate limiting. websocket service uses GENERATED_COOKIE session affinity + 24h timeout.
patterns.genericization: Reusable iGaming edge-security pattern: route allowlist (unknown path -> 404), OWASP preconfigured rule set, geo-blocking, IP rate-limit that whitelists wallet/settlement paths so legitimate high-frequency play isn't throttled, session affinity for realtime/websocket. Feeds comp-* (compliance/geo) and deploy edge-security skills.

patterns.name: Version-pinned microservice deployment via single versions map + per-service migration Jobs
patterns.summary: All deployable services + game servers are declared with image tags in one `versions` map per env (main.tf). app.tf builds k8s Deployment+Service+HPA+BackendConfig per service from a single locals map (uniform /health readiness+liveness probes, rolling update maxUnavailable=0, resource requests/limits, config-map + secret env injection). DB migrations are NOT run on startup — they run as dedicated Kubernetes Jobs whose re-run is gated by a terraform_data trigger on the service version (replace_triggered_by), with backoff_limit=0, restart=Never, and long statement/lock timeouts. Infra changes must be applied BEFORE service version bumps (separate PRs).
patterns.genericization: Reusable deploy model: declarative version map -> templated Deployment/Service/HPA loop; health-probe + zero-downtime rolling defaults; migrations as version-gated one-shot Jobs (idempotent re-run only on version change) decoupled from app boot; ordering rule infra-PR-before-version-PR; dev-before-prod gate. Strong material for deploy-* and migration skills.

patterns.name: Keyless CI/CD via Workload Identity Federation with split least-privilege providers
patterns.summary: GitHub Actions authenticates to GCP via WIF (OIDC) — no JSON keys in CI. Two providers in one pool per project: a narrow one (github-provider) for kubectl rollout restart only, and a broader one (github-tf-provider) for terraform plan/apply. Prod apply gated to main branch via GitHub Environment branch policies (provider condition only scopes to repo). terraform-plan runs on PRs across all 4 envs (matrix, fail-fast:false) and posts the plan back as a PR comment; terraform-apply runs dev first then prod (needs:), concurrency group prevents overlap, then a reusable workflow rewrites the README versions table via a GitHub App token. A manual restart-deployment workflow does targeted kubectl rollout restart + rollout status wait.
patterns.genericization: Reusable CI/CD blueprint: OIDC/WIF keyless auth, separate least-privilege identities for restart-vs-apply, plan-on-PR-with-comment, dev-gates-prod sequencing, branch-policy prod gating, auto-doc of deployed versions. Vendor-neutral (swap GCP WIF for AWS OIDC). Feeds deploy-* and CI skills. NOTE GAP: the standalone deploy/ dir contradicts this by holding long-lived SA JSON keys.

patterns.name: Log-based metrics + transaction dashboards from structured logs
patterns.summary: Observability is built by extracting metrics from structured JSON logs: finished_transactions / failed_transactions counters and transaction_response_time / response_time DISTRIBUTIONs, each labeled by wallet, operator, brand, game, env, error code/reason — extracted via EXTRACT(jsonPayload.data.*) label extractors with exponential histogram buckets. Dashboards chart per-wallet response times (p50/p95 with 300ms baseline + 1000ms critical thresholds), finished/failed transaction rates, RGS /game/play end-to-end latency p50/p95/p99 (5s warning + 40s timeout threshold lines), RGS pod restart count, and exceptions by message. Alerts: uptime check on /health/all (NOT_CONTAINS 'OFFLINE'), primary DB CPU>80%, fanned out to SMS+email+Slack.
patterns.genericization: Reusable iGaming observability spec: emit structured logs with stable fields (wallet/operator/brand/game/correlationId/responseTime), derive counter+distribution metrics by those labels, and standard dashboards (per-wallet latency, transaction success/failure rate, end-to-end round latency with SLO threshold lines, service restart count). The incident proves the key rule: track end-to-end round latency, not just the outbound HTTP call. Feeds qa-* / monitoring / SLO skills.

patterns.name: Layered env/config management: config-map vs secret vs per-service env merge
patterns.summary: Three config tiers: a shared k8s ConfigMap (ENV, NODE_ENV, DB_HOST/PORT, REPLICA_DB_HOST, base-currency + decimals, log level, CORS, archive window) applied to all services; a shared Secret (DB creds, JWT_SECRET, Redis); and per-service env-vars merged from a base map + var.env-vars override (lookup with default {}). Cross-service discovery is by k8s DNS (http://rgs/graphql, http://promo/graphql). Shared HMAC-style ADAPTER_RGS_KEY (random_password) authenticates RGS<->adapter. Game-server pods set remove-secrets=true so they never receive DB/JWT secrets (only mail). Runtime secrets live in GCP Secret Manager, container created by TF but value populated manually then read via data source.
patterns.genericization: Reusable config taxonomy: non-secret shared config-map, shared secret bundle, per-service overridable env map (base + override merge with safe default), internal-DNS service discovery, shared service-to-service auth key, and least-secret-exposure for stateless/game pods. Secret-Manager-create-empty-then-populate avoids secrets in git/state. Feeds config-management and security skills.

patterns.name: Input validation at the game boundary before any RNG draw
patterns.summary: Mini-games validate all client-supplied params (existence, type, integer/finite, discrete-choice range, enum for difficulty) BEFORE making any RNG call or touching the wallet, throwing explicit errors. Multiplayer games additionally do belt-and-suspenders command validation, and wrap the entire tick() in try/catch that on fatal error refunds all stakes, cancels pending commands, resets state, ends the round, and broadcasts an error event.
patterns.genericization: Generic boundary-validation + safe-failure rule: validate-before-randomness/validate-before-debit; on unrecoverable error, refund stakes and reset rather than leave money in limbo. Vendor-neutral correctness/integrity guidance for build-* and qa-* skills.

patterns.name: Monorepo: thin game apps over one versioned shared library
patterns.summary: pnpm workspace (apps/* + packages/*) + Turborepo. Every game is a Vite app in apps/<game>/ that imports a single shared @studio library; CLAUDE.md mandates 'no game logic in the shared package, no shared code duplicated into games'. Shared library is sliced into store/ api/ hooks/ utils/ constants/ ui/ providers/. There is an explicit _mini-game-template/ app used as the scaffold for new games.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'monorepo-game-platform-structure' — pnpm+Turborepo workspace with apps/<game> thin apps over a single versioned @platform/core package (host bridge, money math, fairness UI, stores, autobet, audio). Ship a _game-template app as the new-game generator. Hard rule: core stays game-agnostic; per-game code never leaks into core.

patterns.name: Centralized shared-config packages (no per-app config drift)
patterns.summary: Dedicated workspace packages for every cross-cutting config: @repo/eslint-config, @platform/tailwind-config, @platform/typescript-config (base.json + react-library.json + vite.json), @platform/vitest-config (base/global-setup/setup-files), plus @platform/translations (21-lang) and a vendored @platform/mw-number-flow animation fork. Apps extend these rather than re-declaring.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'shared-config-packages' — extract eslint/prettier/tsconfig/tailwind/vitest into named workspace packages so a single edit re-bases every app. Prettier house style is encoded inside the eslint-config (tabs width 4, no semi, single quote, jsxSingleQuote, trailingComma none, LF).

patterns.name: Config-driven conditional build/deploy pipeline
patterns.summary: A single games-config.json declares per-environment (dev/prod) game lists, source dirs, build variants, and parent→sub-app relationships. Bash helpers (load-games-config.sh, detect-changes.sh, detect-pr-changes.sh, conditional-build.sh, conditional-deploy.sh) diff git tags: packages/* change → rebuild all; app dir change → rebuild that app + sub-apps; otherwise skip. Bitbucket Pipelines is tag-driven (push dev-*/prod-* tag triggers build+deploy of that commit regardless of branch).
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'conditional-monorepo-ci' — declarative <project>-config.json + change-detection scripts so CI only rebuilds affected apps; tag-driven deploy (env-prefixed tags) with a documented frozen-branch rollback strategy. Reusable as a generic 'detect-changed-workspaces-between-tags' script.

patterns.name: RTP-variant build system (one source → N payout variants)
patterns.summary: Instead of duplicating an app per RTP (legacy aviator+aviator0), apps/<game>/rtp.config.json declares variants {gameCode, rtpPercentage, buildSuffix, urlPath}; vite reads RTP_VARIANT env, bakes VITE_GAME_CODE + VITE_RTP_PERCENTAGE via define, rewrites the CDN urlPath, and emits build / build-rtp<pct>. Source bundle is byte-identical across variants; backend picks the RTP table purely from the reported gameCode. Legacy GDK equivalent: alternate game codes (limbo0, dragon-tower0 rtp=1) selected at runtime.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'rtp-variant-builds' (feeds slot-math-designer / balance-check) — build N RTP/house-edge variants from one codebase via an env-driven gameCode/rtp swap; server owns the math table, client only displays the advertised percentage. Generic 'build-matrix-from-config-json' pattern.

patterns.name: Standardized host-embed bridge contract (window.<host>game)
patterns.summary: Games mount INLINE into the host's <div id='mw-mini-game'> via createRoot (no iframe/postMessage/window.parent — confirmed by grep across both generations). Contract = a global bridge object (setupMwGameFunctions) installed exactly once (guarded), re-installed on refreshGameState; host→game methods (unmountReactApp, updateConfigData, refreshGameState, updateUserToken, getFiatCurrencyRates, fairness controls) and game→host CustomEvents on a private EventTarget (balanceUpdated[native], gameSessionUpdated, betAmount/winAmount telemetry). Config resolved URL-query-first-else-data-attr.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'casino-host-embed-bridge' — define a stable window.<host>game inline-mount contract (install-once guard, re-install on remount, URL-query>data-attr config resolution, native-balance broadcast + session bracketing) that is held wire-stable across framework rewrites so the host loads any generation interchangeably.

patterns.name: Provable-fairness commit-reveal model (server-authoritative)
patterns.summary: One scheme both generations: server-authoritative commit-reveal HMAC-SHA256. Client never generates outcomes — submits/rotates a client seed, reads serverSeedHash/nonce, calls /game/proveFairness to re-derive outcome for display. Single-player {clientSeed, serverSeedHash, nextServerSeedHash, nonce}; multiplayer {hashIndex, hash, seed}. Provider routing (tequity default vs a hardcoded metawin set) is a recurring latent bug. Per-game decoder reads a gameEvent field; rotation blocked while unfinishedGames>0.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'provable-fairness-verifier' — client-side commit-reveal verify UI + per-game gameEvent decoder over a server-authoritative HMAC-SHA256 prover; centralize provider routing in one table to avoid silent prover breakage; gate verify UI fields by single- vs multi-player.

patterns.name: Decimal-safe money math + native/fiat display split
patterns.summary: mathOperations(value, decimalPlaces) is a decimal.js builder (add/subtract/multiply/divide/sum + convertToNative/FiatValue with rounding) — the mandated money-mutation path to avoid float drift. INVARIANT: server money is always native, fiat is display-only; precision is server-driven (currencyDecimals from /authenticate, not a static per-currency table); live FX rates pushed by host. player.store carries parallel native + display fields. Naming gotcha preserved in docs: roundDown() uses ROUND_CEIL.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'igaming-money-math' — decimal.js wrapper for all bet/win/balance arithmetic; native-is-truth / fiat-is-display invariant; read precision from auth, never hardcode; freebet/campaign bets never fiat-converted. Port math-operations.ts verbatim as a starter.

patterns.name: Autobet / strategy (advanced-autobet) engine
patterns.summary: Both generations ship an autobet manager + a richer 'strategy' engine: user-defined conditions (trigger onBet/onProfit, frequency every/everyStreakOf/firstStreakOf/streakGreater/Lower, frequencyTrigger win/lose/bet) → actions (increase/decrease/add/subtract/set/reset bet, stopAutoBet), evaluated each round result {won, profit, loss, balance}, bet amount clamped to min/max via mathOperations. Newer games tolerate AUTOBET_ERROR_THRESHOLD=3 consecutive errors; studio stop is cooperative (let in-flight animation finish).
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'autobet-strategy-engine' — config-driven condition→action bet automation evaluated on each round outcome, with bet-bounds clamping, profit/loss/balance stop conditions, and N-consecutive-error tolerance. strategy-manager.ts is a near-portable artifact (strip its console.logs).

patterns.name: Round lifecycle as a TanStack Query mutation FSM (studio) vs Signals+Redux (GDK)
patterns.summary: Studio: each round is a usePlayMutation with onMutate(startGameSession, hide popups, disable popovers) → onSuccess(setBalanceFromApi, store round, animate, completeGame, max-win detect via finalWin<wager.win, win/loss stats after animationDelay) → onError(endGameSession FIRST, then open error modal). GDK: mutable singletons (gameStateData/gameConfigData/gameData) as source of truth + Redux UI mirror + synchronous Signals bus (gameStarted/betPlacing/betPlaced/roundCompleted) + Gateway/Parser/Server subclasses per game.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'game-round-lifecycle' — model a bet round as idle→startSession→server→animate→complete→endSession→idle; always endGameSession before surfacing errors so the host knows the balance was released even on failure. Document both the modern mutation-FSM and the legacy Signals+singleton+Redux variant.

patterns.name: Swappable AI-asset provider abstraction → engine-ready sprite atlas (ludo.ai)
patterns.summary: Single SpriteAnimationProvider interface (generate(request)→GeneratedAnimation) is the only seam; a registry factory + a pure providerCatalog (UI metadata: id/label/badge/tagline/status ready|beta/needsToken/docsUrl) turn (providerId, token) into a concrete provider with an exhaustiveness never-guard. HttpProvider base holds baseUrl + authHeaders + in-memory-only key (never logged/persisted) and throws an actionable ProviderError until a live endpoint is confirmed. Mock-first: whole pipeline runs against a fake provider, real APIs (ludo/scenario/pixellab) drop in as adapters. Pure packing (maxrects-packer) + buildPixiAtlasJson emit TexturePacker JSON-Hash that PixiJS v8 Spritesheet parses, with a per-animation frame-name convention 'walk/000'.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'ai-asset-pipeline' (feeds game-art / asset-audit / setup-engine) — mock-first swappable AI-generation provider behind one interface + UI catalog metadata + secure in-memory key handling, producing engine-ready (PixiJS/TexturePacker) sprite-sheet atlases. Pure layout/atlas-JSON stays DOM-free and unit-tested; canvas/IO isolated in thin glue. Highly portable.

patterns.name: Immutability + small-files + boundary-validation house style (enforced in code & docs)
patterns.summary: ludo.ai's shared types.ts opens with codebase-wide rules: treat all types IMMUTABLE (never mutate, return new objects), keep pure logic DOM-free for testability, isolate canvas/IO in thin glue. Reducer documents 'same reference for unknown action'. Every pure builder validates its inputs at the boundary with explicit throw-on-malformed asserts. Mirrors the user's global coding-style rules. NOTE the explicit counter-example: the GDK generation deliberately MUTATES singletons in place (immutability convention does NOT apply there) — a documented exception.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'game-code-house-style' — immutable domain types, pure-logic-separated-from-IO, boundary asserts on every pure builder, exhaustiveness never-guards on unions. Capture both the modern immutable convention AND the legacy mutable-singleton exception so agents don't 'fix' intentional mutation.

patterns.name: Test conventions: *.unit.spec + Playwright e2e harness with per-game projects, WS mocks, MSW fixtures
patterns.summary: Unit tests named *.unit.spec.{ts,tsx} (uniform coverage gathering); Testing-Library get/query/find selector-intent table; query data mocked via queryClientMock.setQueryData. e2e/ is its own pnpm package: Playwright with per-game projects, base/page-object fixtures, mocked + real-api fixture pairs, MSW mock-server, dedicated WebSocket mocks (aviator/slide/sicbo), and reusable scenario mocks (error-500, network-timeout, slow-network, recovery-active) + canned data (auth/balance/complete/recover/campaigns). CLAUDE.md notes coverage is thin/uneven across games.
patterns.genericization: Skill: 'game-test-harness' — uniform *.unit.spec naming + TanStack-Query mock helpers + a standalone Playwright e2e package with per-game projects, mocked/real fixture pairs, WS mocks, and reusable network-failure & recovery scenario mocks. Port the scenario-mock + fixture-pair structure as a starter.

turbo.json (tasks: build/lint/dev; dependsOn ^build; outputs dist/**; cache:false on build/dev)

asset-manifest.json (build output): {files:{'main.js':cdnUrl,'main.css':cdnUrl}, entrypoints:[], timestamp} + .headers no-cache sidecar; Vite IIFE single bundle, static/js|css|media/[name]-[hash]

REST host contract (uris.ts): /authenticate, /game/{info,play,complete,recover,proveFairness}, /fairness/{activeRngSeeds,roundRngState,updateClientSeed}, /balance, /campaigns

PixiJS/TexturePacker JSON-Hash atlas (ludo.ai): {frames:{'<anim>/<NNN>':{frame,spriteSourceSize,sourceSize,rotated,trimmed}}, meta:{image,format:'RGBA8888',size,scale:'1'}, animations:{name:[orderedFrameKeys]}}

gaps: No server/backend source in any repo — RTP tables, HMAC→outcome distributions (e.g. crash-point max(1,0.99/(1-H))), and per-tier probability tables live server-side and cannot be verified from clients; slot-math-designer/balance-check skills will need server math captured separately (GAME-ANALYSIS §7 flags these as the largest open questions).

Test coverage is thin and uneven: most studio apps and most GDK games have ZERO game-specific tests; high-value math models (Dice/Mines/Dragon-Tower/Diamonds paytables) are untested. The *.unit.spec convention exists but isn't broadly applied — a testing-patterns skill should treat the convention, not the existing coverage, as the source of truth.

Production console.log leakage: strategy-manager.ts and many studio play hooks (e.g. apps/bars/src/hooks/use-play.ts) log bet/win/balance; relies on Vite drop_console which several call sites are flagged as needing verification. Any ported artifact must strip these.

GDK generation is copy-pasted and DRIFTED across repos (connector.ts/gateway.ts/gameConfigData.ts/strategyBetManager.ts differ; HTTP timeout 10s vs 30s for Plinko; currency allow-lists disagree). No single canonical GDK source exists — genericizing the legacy track requires reconciling divergent copies, and only one GDK repo (chao-refacting-version/Dice) was directly on disk here.

Fairness provider routing (tequity vs hardcoded metawin set) is duplicated per-game and silently breaks /proveFairness when wrong — needs centralizing; the correct membership for some games (chase, hexshot) is unverified.

ludo.ai real providers (ludo/scenario/pixellab) are 'beta' stubs — generate() throws until live endpoints are confirmed; no server proxy/job-queue yet (needed to hide keys and respect ludo.ai's 1-concurrent-request limit). Only the Mock path is end-to-end functional.

RTP-variant opt-in is partial: only aviator, roulette, roulette-pro currently use rtp.config.json; the GDK track has no build system for variants (runtime alternate game codes instead) — two divergent mechanisms to unify.

No explicit shared asset-naming/file-size-budget standard doc was found in the monorepo (CLAUDE.md gives SVG+SVGR + per-app public/ rules only); asset-audit skill lacks a codified budget/naming spec to enforce against.
